Rule: Same-data BAO best-fit baselines cannot be the primary K2 scoring baseline.
Reason: Prevents using a posterior maximum or scale correction learned from the tested BAO vector as a locked prediction baseline.
FailureMode: K2 appears artificially compatible because the baseline already absorbed the BAO residual.
NextAction: Use same-data baselines only as calibration/sensitivity controls.

Rule: At least one independent or external baseline must be scored before K2 interpretation.
Reason: Separates baseline fit quality from finite-memory correction behavior.
FailureMode: K2 correction is interpreted on a baseline that is already data-tuned.
NextAction: Use CMB-only or another public external baseline as the first independent bracket.

Rule: K2 and all null comparators must use the same residual vector and covariance.
Reason: Prevents comparing scores across incompatible residual spaces.
FailureMode: Apparent K2 advantage comes from changing covariance or diagnostic target.

Rule: K2 cannot exceed rho<=4 and cannot change p=3.
NextAction: Flag as strong negative if compatibility requires rho>4 or p change.

Rule: Report same-data best-fit, rd-offset sensitivity, CMB-only baseline, and audit baseline side by side.
Reason: Shows whether K2 behavior depends on permissive or independent baselines.
NextAction: Update evidence/bao_baseline_scorecard.csv before any K2 comparison.

Rule: No baseline score can be called measurement validation until K2/null protocol is registered.
FailureMode: Diagnostic preflight is overstated as measurement result.
NextAction: Create K2 protocol registry and readiness check first.
